text.title: langchain-basics Failure Modes
text.summary: Four failure modes with symptom, root cause, and repair.

section.title: Antipatterns

antipattern.description: Using `JsonOutputParser(pydantic_object=...)` for downstream code that depends on specific fields.
antipattern.reason: Parser fails silently on malformed JSON from smaller models; downstream consumers see partial dicts.

section.p: Symptom: KeyError or missing-field errors downstream; sporadic on small-model paths. Root cause: prompt-based parsing. Fix: switch to `model.with_structured_output(Model)` for function-calling-backed parsing.

antipattern.reason: Minor version bumps change interfaces; production breaks unpredictably across redeploys.

section.p: Symptom: redeploy fails with AttributeError on imports that worked yesterday. Root cause: unpinned. Fix: pin to exact versions; bump explicitly via PR with changelog review.

antipattern.reason: Parallel dict keys execute synchronously in CPython; no actual concurrency without async.

section.p: Symptom: total time = sum of branches, not max. Root cause: misconception. Fix: use `asyncio.gather()` over `chain.ainvoke` per element; document the sync RunnableParallel as parallel-shape-only.

antipattern.description: Using `InMemoryCache` for LLM responses in a multi-worker deployment.
antipattern.reason: Cache is process-local; cache hit rate stays low because each worker rebuilds its own.

section.p: Symptom: cache hit rate near zero despite repetitive queries. Root cause: in-process cache. Fix: use `RedisCache` or equivalent shared cache; or accept low hit rate as a known property.
